topic.what: Survey of Blockchain-enabled Federated Learning (BCFL) architectures combining decentralized ML with tamper-proof ledgers
topic.how: Four-dimensional taxonomy (coordination/consensus/storage/trust) with comparative analysis of MORFLB and FBCI-SHS frameworks and dual-objective optimization formulation
topic.why_matters: Addresses privacy violations and single-point failures in centralized ML for IoT/transports/healthcare by enabling collaborative training without raw data sharing

characteristics.context: CIFAR-100 classification; lower than typical centralized due to FL overhead

characteristics.what_it_measures: Average client execution latency per federated round
characteristics.evidence: Table II comparison with 5 baseline methods
characteristics.context: Multi-objective RL scheduler to minimize delay while maximizing rewards

characteristics.what_it_measures: Tamper-proof integrity of model updates via blockchain verification
characteristics.evidence: PoW hashing validates all model updates across distributed clients
characteristics.context: Normalized score; 1.0 indicates full tamper-proof verification achieved

characteristics.evidence: Table II; smart contracts enforce data governance rules
characteristics.context: Emerging metric; limited validation in paper

characteristics.what_itasures: Model update validation rate via blockchain consensus
characteristics.evidence: 11 blocks mined in 0.121s average per block; PoW difficulty=2

characteristics.context: Non-IID data slows convergence; no IID baseline for comparison

good_for: privacy-critical healthcare IoT with strict data sovereignty
intelligent transportation systems requiring tamper-proof threat detection
multi-stakeholder environments needing verifiable model update provenance
not_for: ultra-low-latency applications where centralized approaches suffice
large-scale deployments with 100+ concurrent clients due to communication overhead
bandwidth-constrained IoT networks where model updates (100MB-1GB) are prohibitive
applications requiring deterministic finality (PBFT more appropriate than PoW)
requires: edge devices with sufficient compute for local training and RL scheduling
stable network connectivity for blockchain consensus and federated rounds
non-IID data handling capability (Dirichlet-based partitioning tested)
Permissioned or consortium trust model for production deployment
